title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: March 25, 2026 - Navigating Geopolitical Shifts and Economic Data

sections.title: Market Pulse: Cautious Optimism Amidst Geopolitical Hopes

items.headline: U.S. Futures Surge on Hopes of U.S.-Iran De-escalation, Oil Prices Fall
items.summary: U.S. stock futures advanced significantly on Wednesday, March 25, driven by optimism surrounding potential peace negotiations between the U.S. and Iran. This sentiment led to a sharp decline in oil prices, easing concerns over energy supply disruptions. However, overall market sentiment remains one of cautious optimism amidst lingering economic uncertainty and central bank policy considerations.

items.statContext: Up 0.99% on Wednesday; Futures up 0.89% in pre-market. Closed down 0.37% on Tuesday at 6,556.37.
Nasdaq 100 futures gained 0.8% in pre-market. Closed down 0.84% on Tuesday at 21,761.89.
Dow futures rose about 0.7% in pre-market. Closed down 0.18% on Tuesday at 46,124.06.

items.headline: Tilly's, Inc. Leads Monthly Gains
items.summary: Tilly's, Inc. is the top-performing stock for March, with a significant monthly gain, reflecting strong momentum.

items.headline: Sable Offshore Corp. Sees Substantial March Increase
items.summary: Sable Offshore Corp. experienced a nearly 100% surge in March, positioning it among the top monthly gainers.

items.headline: Arm Holdings Jumps on New AI Chip Announcement
items.summary: Arm Holdings saw a significant pre-market rise after unveiling a new AI data center chip expected to generate billions in revenue.

items.headline: Outlook Therapeutics Tumbles After Share Offering
items.summary: Outlook Therapeutics shares plummeted following the pricing of a public offering of shares and warrants.

items.headline: Estée Lauder Slumps Amid Merger Discussions
items.summary: Estée Lauder's stock declined after the company confirmed discussions regarding a potential business combination with Puig.

items.headline: Chewy Misses Q4 Earnings Estimates
items.summary: Chewy reported Q4 earnings of $0.27 per share, falling short of the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.28 per share, contributing to a year-to-date decline.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained the federal funds rate at 3.5%-3.75% during its March 18, 2026 meeting. The FOMC acknowledged elevated uncertainty regarding the economic outlook due to developments in the Middle East and their potential implications for the U.S. economy. Markets currently anticipate no change in rates for the April meeting.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Releases on March 25th
items.summary: Today's economic calendar includes the release of U.S. Trade Price Indices for February, U.S. Current Account for Q4, and U.S. Import and Export Price Indexes for February. The U.K. also released its CPI for February. These data points provide insights into inflation trends and international trade balances.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Sees Value Outperforming Growth, Consumer Durables Lead in India
items.summary: A noticeable sector rotation is underway, with value stocks outperforming growth in 2026. Geopolitical risks are shifting focus away from some technology and AI sectors towards 'safer havens'. In the Indian market, the Nifty Consumer Durables index led gains at 3.51% on Wednesday, with significant gains also seen in real estate and PSU banks.

items.headline: Gold and Silver Rebound, But Monthly Declines Persist
items.summary: Gold prices rebounded on Wednesday, surging over 3%, while silver prices saw a more than 5% increase. Despite today's gains, gold is still down over 13% for March, and silver has corrected more than 20% this month, reflecting high volatility amidst global uncertainty. Crude oil futures were trading lower at approximately $86.93 per barrel on Wednesday morning.
